summary: Fragmentary audience tape with modest fidelity; the set is incomplete and historically oversold, leaving no clear musical highlights.
review: The archive recording is an audience‑sourced mono capture that starts mid‑set (after “Me and My Uncle”) and omits the opening of “Cold Rain and Snow,” so listeners only get a partial performance. Reviewers focus more on the chaotic, oversold concert circumstances than on any standout musical moments, noting that the show was likely cut short by authorities.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers criticize the incomplete, low‑fidelity source and the chaotic event context; a few give moderate scores, yielding an overall low‑to‑mid rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Recorded on a cheap portable Panasonic mono tape recorder; missing the first part of the set and limited to a narrow frequency range, resulting in modest clarity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert was heavily oversold, possibly cut short by fire officials, and the archived audio begins after the opening number, making the set appear truncated and disrupting any natural flow.
